question.text: What minimum net worth was recommended by the RBI-SEBI Standing Technical Committee for a recognised Stock Exchange to set up a Currency Futures segment?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The RBI-SEBI Standing Technical Committee "suggested a separate segment for currency futures with separate trading platform, clearing corporation and membership from other segments. The Exchange shall have balance sheet net worth of at least Rs. 100 crores." (Now codified under SEBI (Stock Exchange and Clearing Corporation) Regulation, 2018.) **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore is the net worth for trading members; Rs. 10 crores is for clearing members. Rs. 500 crores is the net worth for AD Category-I banks to become TMs/CMs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Three different net worth thresholds from the Committee: Exchange = Rs. 100 crores; Clearing Member = Rs. 10 crores; Trading Member = Rs. 1 crore. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Memorize the trio: Exchange Rs. 100 Cr > Clearing Member Rs. 10 Cr > Trading Member Rs. 1 Cr.
